name: skills-creator
description: Creates and maintains Agent Skills with effective triggers and progressive disclosure. Use when user requests to create a skill, generate a SKILL.md, build custom capabilities, or mentions "create skill", "new skill", or "skill configuration".
capabilities: Reads best practices documentation to understand current skill authoring guidelines
Validates skill names for length, character set, and reserved word restrictions
Generates SKILL.md files with proper YAML frontmatter and markdown structure
Organizes skill resources in appropriate folder structures
author: Emz1998
securitySummary: All 429 static findings are FALSE POSITIVES. The skill is a documentation-driven meta-skill that reads reference files and creates SKILL.md files. The static analyzer incorrectly flagged code examples within markdown documentation as security issues. No actual shell commands, cryptographic operations, or system reconnaissance occur at runtime.
valueStatement: Creating Agent Skills manually requires understanding complex formatting rules and best practices. This skill automates skill creation by reading reference documentation, validating inputs, and generating properly-structured SKILL.md files following Claude Code conventions.
limitations: Does not execute user commands or modify system files outside the skill creation workflow
Does not create or run scripts within generated skills
Does not install dependencies or configure development environments
Does not integrate with external APIs or network services

useCases.targetUser: Developers extending Claude Code capabilities for specific domains
useCases.title: New Skill Development
useCases.description: A developer wants to create a new Agent Skill for PDF processing. They ask the skill to create a skill, and it generates a properly formatted SKILL.md with workflow steps, rules, and acceptance criteria.

useCases.targetUser: Team leads establishing shared skill libraries
useCases.title: Team Skill Standardization
useCases.description: A team lead wants all team skills to follow consistent naming conventions and documentation patterns. The skill ensures new skills match existing team standards.

useCases.targetUser: Plugin authors creating skill packages for distribution
useCases.title: Plugin Skill Creation
useCases.description: A plugin developer needs to bundle multiple related skills with consistent structure. The skill creates uniform SKILL.md files across the skill collection.

promptTemplates.title: Basic Skill Creation
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a new Agent Skill called [skill-name] that [what it does]. Use when [when to use it].

promptTemplates.title: Detailed Skill with Rules
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a skill called [skill-name] for [purpose]. Include workflow steps: 1) [step 1], 2) [step 2], 3) [step 3]. Add rules: [rule 1], [rule 2]. Use when [triggers].
promptTemplates.scenario: Complex skill requiring specific operational procedures

promptTemplates.title: Restricted Tools Skill
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a read-only skill called [skill-name] for [purpose]. Allowed tools: Read, Grep, Glob. Use when [trigger conditions].
promptTemplates.scenario: Skill requiring limited tool access for security

promptTemplates.title: Multi-File Skill
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a skill called [skill-name] with supporting files: [reference file] for [purpose], [scripts folder] for [purpose]. Include instructions for loading additional files.
promptTemplates.scenario: Complex skill requiring reference documentation and utility scripts

bestPractices: Use specific trigger phrases in descriptions to help Claude discover the skill when relevant
Keep SKILL.md under 500 lines and reference additional files for detailed documentation
Match freedom level to task fragility: high freedom for flexible tasks, low freedom for precise sequences
antiPatterns: Vague descriptions like "processes data" or "helps with files" that prevent skill discovery
Including information Claude already knows or can infer
Creating overly broad skills that should be split into focused, single-purpose skills

faq.question: Where should I store the skills I create?
faq.answer: Personal skills go in ~/.claude/skills/. Project skills go in .claude/skills/ within your project directory. Project skills are shared via git.

faq.question: What characters are allowed in skill names?
faq.answer: Skill names must use only lowercase letters, numbers, and hyphens. Maximum length is 64 characters. Reserved words like 'anthropic' and 'claude' cannot be used.

faq.question: How does Claude discover my skills?
faq.answer: Claude reads skill names and descriptions at startup. It selects relevant skills based on your request matching the description. Specific trigger phrases improve discovery.

faq.question: Can I restrict which tools a skill can use?
faq.answer: Yes. Add the allowed-tools field to the YAML frontmatter listing specific tools. Claude will only use those tools when this skill is active.

faq.question: How do I test if my skill works correctly?
faq.answer: Ask a question that matches your skill description. Claude will autonomously activate the skill. Use specific trigger terms from your description.

faq.question: What should I include in the skill description?
faq.answer: Describe what the skill does and when to use it. Include specific keywords users might mention. Write in third person voice without 'I' or 'you'.
